meta.title: 3 Days Best of Kota Kinabalu + Kundasang | Dhesu Travel Malaysia
meta.description: 3-day Sabah tour: Rumah Terbalik, Kinabalu National Park (UNESCO), Desa Cow Farm, Sosodikon Hill sunrise, Floating Mosque and KK city highlights. From RM1,098 per person. 100% private, daily departures. Dhesu Travel, trusted since 1988.
meta.keywords: Kota Kinabalu Kundasang tour package Malaysia, Sabah tour package from KL, Kinabalu Park tour package, Kundasang highland tour Malaysia, Desa Farm Sabah tour, Rumah Terbalik Sabah, KK tour itinerary Malaysia, Sabah Borneo package

hero.title: 3 Days Best of Kota Kinabalu
hero.intro: From a suspension bridge over a jungle ravine to the only Upside Down House in Southeast Asia. From a UNESCO World Heritage primary rainforest to a mountain dairy farm where Jersey calves graze beneath the highest peak in Southeast Asia. This is Sabah's greatest hits — the landmarks, the highlands, the wildlife encounters, the cultural markets and the coastal sunsets — compressed into three extraordinary days entirely on your terms. Kota Kinabalu and Kundasang are two completely different worlds, separated by 80 kilometres of mountain road and about 1,500 metres of altitude. This package shows you both.
hero.heroImageAlt: Kundasang highland Sabah Mount Kinabalu view Desa Farm morning mist

overview.heading: 3 Days Covering the Full Sabah Sweep — City, Highlands & Heritage
body: This three-day Sabah tour is the most comprehensive short itinerary available for Kota Kinabalu and Kundasang. It covers the coastal city's architectural oddities and cultural markets, the Tamparuli jungle suspension bridge, Nabalu's famous indigenous craft market, the UNESCO World Heritage Kinabalu National Park, the highland village world of Kundasang with its Sosodikon Hill sunrise, the remarkable Desa Cow Farm and Ranau Rabbit Farm, the river fish therapy at Bombon Marakau Village, the KK Floating Mosque at sunset, and the final morning's sweep through the city's museums, jewellery district and food souvenirs.

The itinerary is structured around altitude: Day 1 climbs from sea level Kota Kinabalu up through the foothills and into the Kinabalu highlands, with an overnight in Kundasang at 1,500 metres elevation. The cool mountain air, the mist over the Crocker Range, and the views of Mount Kinabalu — the highest peak between the Himalayas and the mountains of Papua New Guinea — make the highland overnight essential to the experience. Day 2 descends back to KK through more highland attractions before reaching the coast for the famous Tanjung Aru beach sunset. Day 3 is an unhurried city morning before the airport.

This package is 100% private throughout — your driver-guide, your vehicle, your pace. Group pricing tiers (3+ adults, 5+ adults, 7+ adults) make it the most cost-effective option for family groups and friends travelling together. It departs daily and is valid until 31 March 2027.

highlights.heading: Why This KK + Kundasang Package Stands Apart

items.title: Kinabalu National Park — UNESCO World Heritage Rainforest Nature Trail
items.body: Gazetted as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2000, Kinabalu National Park covers 754 square kilometres and protects one of the world's richest and most important biological sites. The park contains over 4,500 species of flora — including 1,000 orchid species, nine of the world's pitcher plant species, and the Rafflesia, the world's largest flower. The guided nature trail through the park's primary rainforest takes you beneath the canopy of trees that have stood for centuries, past mountain streams and through a forest that transitions from lowland tropical to montane oak forest as altitude increases. Mount Kinabalu itself — at 4,095 metres the highest peak in Southeast Asia outside the Himalayas — towers above the treeline when cloud allows. The park headquarters at 1,558 metres is the starting point, and the nature trail is accessible without special permits or climbing experience.
items.imageAlt: Kinabalu National Park UNESCO rainforest nature trail Sabah Malaysia

items.title: Rumah Terbalik (Upside Down House) — The First of Its Kind in Southeast Asia
items.body: Built entirely upside down — the roof at ground level, the floor above — Rumah Terbalik is one of only five Upside Down Houses in the world and the first ever built in Southeast Asia. Every room inside is inverted: furniture is fixed to the ceiling-as-floor, the kitchen is above your head, and staircases descend from what should be the top of the building. The disorienting visual effect is thoroughly convincing — walking through an entirely normal-looking interior where everything defies gravity creates a genuinely surreal experience. It is an attraction that photographs brilliantly and is a crowd favourite for families. Located on the road toward Tamparuli, it is a natural first stop en route to the highlands.
items.imageAlt: Rumah Terbalik Upside Down House Sabah Malaysia first in Southeast Asia

items.title: Desa Cow Farm — The Little New Zealand of Sabah
items.body: Perched at the foot of Mount Kinabalu near Mesilau at around 1,800 metres elevation, Desa Cow Farm (Ladang Ternakan Desa) manages one of Malaysia's most beloved rural attractions: rolling green paddocks of Jersey dairy cattle with Mount Kinabalu filling the entire skyline behind them. The visual resemblance to New Zealand farmland is so striking that it has earned the nickname 'Little New Zealand of Sabah.' Visitors can bottle-feed calves, taste fresh Desa dairy products (the milk and ice cream are notably good), and spend an hour in the cool highland air with the mountain backdrop for photographs. The contrast between the tropical lowland Sabah and this European-looking dairy landscape is unexpectedly dramatic.
items.imageAlt: Desa Cow Farm Sabah dairy cattle Mount Kinabalu backdrop Little New Zealand

items.title: Sosodikon Hill Sunrise — 400 Metres of Highland Panorama in 10 Minutes
items.body: At 400 metres above the Kundasang valley floor, Sosodikon Hill is one of Sabah's most accessible and most rewarding sunrise viewpoints. The ascent to the peak takes approximately ten minutes on a well-maintained path — brief enough that even non-hikers complete it comfortably — and the view from the top at dawn is extraordinary: Mount Kinabalu's summit emerging from the morning mist while the Kundasang valley below is still submerged in cloud, the highland vegetable farms a patchwork of green in the valley floor. The sunrise quality at Sosodikon depends on cloud cover (cool and misty mornings are typical in Kundasang), but even a partially obscured sunrise from this elevation, in this setting, is a memorable start to any day in Sabah.
items.imageAlt: Sosodikon Hill sunrise Kundasang Sabah Mount Kinabalu morning mist view

days.title: Arrive KK — Tamparuli — Nabalu — Kinabalu Park — Kundasang
days.description: Arrive at Kota Kinabalu International Airport and meet your driver-guide for the start of your Sabah highland journey. The first stop — before leaving the outskirts of KK — is Rumah Terbalik, the Upside Down House: one of only five in the world, the first in Southeast Asia, where every room is inverted and the roof sits at ground level. It is deliberately disorienting and entirely enjoyable. Continue inland to the Jambatan Tamparuli, a 100-metre hanging suspension bridge slung across a jungle river gorge near the town of Tamparuli. The bridge sways with each step, the river races below, and the jungle canopy extends on both sides — a brief but vivid taste of Sabah's interior wilderness. From Tamparuli, the road climbs into the foothills toward Pekan Nabalu and the Nabalu Craft Market, a hilltop market run by Kadazandusun and Rungus indigenous vendors selling handwoven textiles, beaded jewellery, native orchids, mountain vegetables and Sabah honey — the best handicraft market before reaching the highlands. The final approach to Kinabalu National Park headquarters at 1,558 metres gives the first close encounter with the mountain: the summit towers 2,500 metres above, and the transition from lowland tropical heat to crisp mountain air is immediate. Your driver-guide leads a guided nature trail through the park's primary montane rainforest, where pitcher plants cling to the undergrowth and giant oak trees draped in moss filter the afternoon light. Check in at your Kundasang hotel in the late afternoon and spend the evening in the cool highland air — dinner is at your own cost in the Kundasang town area, which has a good range of simple restaurants specialising in fresh highland vegetables and barbecued local meats.
suggestedActivities: KK International Airport arrival
Rumah Terbalik (Upside Down House) — first in Southeast Asia
Jambatan Tamparuli suspension bridge crossing
Pekan Nabalu indigenous craft market
Kinabalu National Park (UNESCO) guided nature trail
Hotel check-in Kundasang (overnight at 1,500m elevation)
Kundasang town dinner at own cost

days.title: Sosodikon Hill Sunrise — Desa Farm — Bombon Marakau — Ranau — KK Sunset
days.description: Rise before dawn for the Sosodikon Hill sunrise experience — your driver will collect you from the hotel early enough to ascend the 400-metre hill in time for first light. The path is straightforward and takes approximately ten minutes to the summit, where Mount Kinabalu's granite peak catches the first rays of the morning sun above the cloud-filled Kundasang valley. Return to the hotel for breakfast before the day's highland exploration continues. The morning heads to Desa Cow Farm (Ladang Ternakan Desa), set at around 1,800 metres elevation near Mesilau with Mount Kinabalu as an improbably dramatic backdrop. The Jersey dairy cattle grazing on the green paddocks, the cool highland mist, and the mountain filling the horizon give the farm its reputation as 'Little New Zealand of Sabah' — a description that undersells how remarkable the landscape actually is. Bottle-feed calves, taste the farm's fresh dairy products, and spend unhurried time in one of Sabah's most photogenic settings. Continue down to the Bombon Marakau Village for river fish therapy at the natural spa pools along the Marakau River, where small doctor fish provide the traditional nibble-exfoliation experience in clear mountain water. The Ranau Rabbit Farm near the town of Ranau is the next stop — unexpectedly charming, with dozens of domestic and ornamental rabbit breeds and the highland valley as a backdrop. The road then descends from the highlands back to KK, passing through the Ranau Valley and stopping at the distinctive PPNS building in the KK waterfront area before proceeding to the Sabah State Mosque's Floating Mosque on the edge of the sea — named for its appearance of floating on the water at high tide, it is among the most architecturally striking mosques in Malaysia. End the day at Tanjung Aru Beach, KK's most celebrated sunset spot, where the water faces due west toward the Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park islands and the sun drops behind the sea in vivid tropical colour. Return to the KK city hotel for the night.
suggestedActivities: Pre-dawn pickup for Sosodikon Hill sunrise hike (10 mins to peak, 400m elevation)
Hotel breakfast
Desa Cow Farm — bottle-feed calves, taste dairy, Mount Kinabalu views
Fish therapy at Bombon Marakau Village river pools
Ranau Rabbit Farm
Return to KK via PPNS building and Sabah Floating Mosque
Tanjung Aru Beach sunset
KK city hotel overnight

days.title: KK City Heritage Morning — Museum — Markets — Departure
days.description: The final morning in Kota Kinabalu is a curated sweep through the city's most rewarding heritage stops before the airport transfer. After breakfast, begin at Museum Negeri Sabah — the Sabah State Museum and Heritage Village — an institution that presents the full natural history and cultural heritage of Sabah's indigenous peoples, including the Kadazandusun, Bajau Laut, Rungus, and Murut communities. The Heritage Village section reconstructs traditional longhouses and village structures from Sabah's major ethnic groups, and the museum's natural history collection includes specimens of the pitcher plants, proboscis monkey skulls and Rafflesia displays from the Kinabalu Park ecosystem. From the museum, continue to Jamilah Jewellery in the city centre — the best-known Sabah pearl jewellery retailer, where South Sea pearls and Sabah freshwater pearls are sold at competitive prices with certificates of authenticity. The Filipino Market (Pasar Filipina) near the Waterfront is Sabah's most vivid open-air bazaar: a covered market of Filipino vendors selling pearl jewellery, dried seafood, batik fabric, and the fresh shellfish that defines KK's coastal food culture. Sabah Choco House is the destination for artisanal Sabah cocoa chocolate — the state is a significant producer of premium cacao and the chocolates here make excellent gifts. The final stop is a Sabah Layer Cake (Kek Lapis Sarawak-style) specialist bakery for the colourful layered pastry that has become one of Sabah and Sarawak's most popular food souvenirs. Transfer to KK International Airport for your return flight to KL — arriving home with a full memory card, Sabah chocolate, pearl jewellery and plans to return for Sipadan.
suggestedActivities: Hotel breakfast
Museum Negeri Sabah — natural history and ethnic heritage collections

Filipino Market (Pasar Filipina) — seafood, pearls, batik
Sabah Choco House — artisanal Sabah cocoa chocolates

whyBook.heading: Sabah's Best Highlands Route — Arranged by the Agency That Has Done It Since 1988
whyBook.body: The KK to Kundasang road is one of the most rewarding drives in Malaysia, but piecing together the Sosodikon Hill sunrise, Desa Farm, Kinabalu Park, the Floating Mosque and the right timing for Tanjung Aru sunset into a single coherent 3-day trip requires knowing exactly which order to do things in. Dhesu has been arranging Sabah packages for Malaysian travellers since 1988. We know the mountain roads, the sunrise conditions at Sosodikon, the Desa Farm opening hours, and the Kinabalu Park permit requirements.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency operating since 1988. All ground arrangements in Sabah are handled by established, vetted local operators with long-standing relationships with Kinabalu Park authorities.

points.title: 35+ Years of Sabah Expertise
points.body: We have arranged highland Sabah tours across every season since 1988. We know when Sosodikon Hill views are clearest, how cloud cover affects Kinabalu Park visits, and how to adjust the itinerary when mountain weather requires flexibility.

points.title: 100% Private — Your Group Only
points.body: Every tour is entirely private. Your certified English-speaking driver-guide and vehicle are exclusively for your group throughout all 3 days. The group discount pricing tiers make this option particularly competitive for families and groups of friends.

points.title: UNESCO Heritage Site Included
points.body: Kinabalu National Park entry, the guided nature trail, and all entrance fees throughout the itinerary are included. There is no separate admission to budget for on arrival.

sections.heading: Kota Kinabalu + Kundasang Tour Package — The Full Highland Sabah Experience
sections.body: The most common mistake Malaysian travellers make when planning a Sabah trip from KL is spending all their time in Kota Kinabalu city without venturing into the Kundasang highlands. KK is an excellent base and a genuinely interesting city, but the highlands — the cool mountain air, the Kinabalu National Park, the Desa Farm landscape, the Sosodikon Hill sunrise — are what make Sabah categorically different from any other Malaysian state. This package solves the problem: it combines both worlds in three days, structuring the itinerary so that nothing significant is missed and the altitude gain and descent are used efficiently.

sections.heading: Kinabalu National Park — UNESCO World Heritage Site Without the Summit Climb
sections.body:
Most travellers know Kinabalu National Park as the base for the Mount Kinabalu summit climb — a 2-day, 1-night permit-required ascent of Southeast Asia's highest peak that requires advance booking months ahead and a reasonable level of physical fitness. This package does not include the summit climb. Instead, the guided nature trail through the park's primary montane rainforest at 1,558 metres gives access to the UNESCO World Heritage forest ecosystem — the pitcher plants, the ancient oak and rhododendron forest, the mountain streams — without the logistics and physical demands of the full summit ascent. It is the right Kinabalu Park experience for travellers who want the World Heritage forest without committing to a two-day climbing expedition.

For travellers who do want to climb Mount Kinabalu, Dhesu can arrange the full summit package separately with advance notice — permits are controlled and bookings fill months in advance, especially for weekend dates.

sections.heading: Kundasang Weather — What to Expect at 1,500 Metres Elevation
sections.body: Kundasang sits at approximately 1,500 metres above sea level in the shadow of Mount Kinabalu, and the temperature difference from KK city (at sea level) is dramatic. Expect daytime temperatures of 18-24°C and night temperatures dropping to 14-18°C — genuinely cool by Malaysian standards. Pack a light jacket or fleece for evenings and the Sosodikon sunrise. The cool air makes Kundasang feel like a complete change of country from the tropical coastal KK below, and many Malaysian visitors find the highland climate one of the most enjoyable aspects of the trip. Morning mist is common throughout the year; clear Mount Kinabalu summit views are most likely in the early morning before cloud builds. December to February tends to bring the most rainfall, while March to September is generally the clearest period for mountain views.

faq.heading: Questions About This 3-Day KK + Kundasang Package

items.question: Are flights from KL to Kota Kinabalu included in the package?
items.answer: No. Flights between Kuala Lumpur and Kota Kinabalu are not included in the package price. AirAsia, Malaysia Airlines and Batik Air operate multiple daily direct flights between KLIA/KLIA2 and KK International Airport, with journey times of approximately 2 hours. We recommend booking flights that arrive at KK before midday on Day 1 to make the most of the first day's itinerary. Dhesu can advise on suitable flight timings and coordinate airport pickup accordingly — contact us before booking your flights.

items.question: Is the Mount Kinabalu summit climb included in this tour?
items.answer: No. This package includes a guided nature trail through Kinabalu National Park at the park headquarters level (1,558 metres elevation) — this is not a summit climb. The nature trail explores the UNESCO World Heritage primary montane rainforest around the park headquarters and is suitable for all fitness levels. The summit climb of Mount Kinabalu (4,095 metres) is a separate 2-day, 1-night expedition requiring advance permits, a guide and porter, and is booked months in advance. If you wish to add the summit climb to your Sabah itinerary, contact Dhesu and we can arrange a longer package incorporating the climb.

items.question: How difficult is the Sosodikon Hill sunrise hike?
items.answer: Sosodikon Hill is very accessible. The ascent to the 400-metre peak takes approximately 10 minutes on a well-maintained path. The gradient is moderate — steeper than a flat walk but nothing requiring hiking boots or trekking poles. The path is suitable for moderately mobile adults including older travellers. Sensible closed-toe footwear with grip is recommended (not flip-flops). The pre-dawn start time (typically departing the hotel around 5:15am) is the main challenge — but the sunrise view from the summit makes the early alarm more than worthwhile.

items.question: What is the best time of year to visit Kundasang and Kinabalu Park?
items.answer: March to September is generally the best period for clear Mount Kinabalu summit views and settled highland weather. October to February brings more cloud and occasional heavier rain, particularly December and January. However, Kinabalu National Park's nature trail is enjoyable year-round — the montane rainforest is at its most lush during and after the wetter months, and the park receives fewer visitors from June to August (Malaysian school holidays aside). Sosodikon Hill sunrise views depend on cloud cover rather than season — arrive and hope for a clear morning; even a misty Kundasang dawn has its own beauty.

items.question: What do Kundasang temperatures feel like — should I pack warm clothes?
items.answer: Yes. Kundasang nights routinely drop to 14-18°C, and the pre-dawn Sosodikon Hill departure will feel genuinely cold by Malaysian standards. Pack at minimum a light fleece or windbreaker for evenings and the sunrise hike. Days are pleasantly cool at 18-24°C — long sleeves are comfortable during Kinabalu Park activities. The temperature contrast from KK city (typically 28-33°C at sea level) to Kundasang (1,500 metres) is the most immediately noticeable part of the highland experience.

items.question: Is this tour suitable for senior travellers and families with young children?
items.answer: Yes, with one consideration. The main itinerary — Rumah Terbalik, Tamparuli Bridge, Nabalu Market, Kinabalu Park nature trail, Desa Farm, Ranau Rabbit Farm, the Floating Mosque, Tanjung Aru Beach, and the KK city stops — is suitable for most ages and fitness levels. The Sosodikon Hill sunrise hike involves a 10-minute uphill walk in pre-dawn darkness; senior travellers with limited mobility may prefer to wait at the trailhead or skip this activity (the hotel is very close and the guide can advise on alternatives). Please inform Dhesu at booking if you have travellers with mobility concerns so we can make appropriate arrangements.
